name: bridge_z3_kernel_ordering
description: Closes the z3 ordering cross-check gap from sim_rustworkx_bridge_dag.py. Three formal z3 UNSAT proofs: (1) dominance transitivity, (2) product state floor, (3) cycle impossibility. All proofs bound to concrete MI/I_c values from the rustworkx DAG.

pytorch.reason: Not needed: metrics loaded from DAG JSON.

z3.reason: Three UNSAT proofs encoding dominance transitivity (P1), product floor (P2), and cycle impossibility (P3). z3 Solver().check() == unsat IS the proof result.

rustworkx.reason: Loads rustworkx_bridge_dag_results.json to bind concrete MI/I_c values to z3 Real variables; directly closes the cross-check gap flagged by the bridge DAG sim.

P1_transitivity.encoding: A_dom_B AND B_dom_C AND NOT(A_dom_C) -> UNSAT
P1_transitivity.note: UNSAT proves: given A dom B and B dom C, the claim NOT(A dom C) is impossible. Dominance transitivity holds for these concrete values.

P2_product_floor.encoding_symbolic: prod_MI=0 AND target_MI>0 AND prod_MI>target_MI -> UNSAT
P2_product_floor.encoding_concrete: product dom Bell on both MI+Ic axes -> UNSAT
P2_product_floor.note: UNSAT proves product (MI=0) cannot have strictly greater MI than any state with MI>0. Product state is a true floor of the dominance partial order.

P3_no_cycle.note: Strict inequality > is irreflexive and asymmetric on the reals. z3 proves directly: no cycle in the MI or I_c ordering is possible.

N1_Bell_dom_Werner07_is_SAT.test: Bell dom Werner-0.7 should be SAT (valid DAG edge)
N1_Bell_dom_Werner07_is_SAT.note: z3 witnesses a true dominance relation — confirms encoding is not trivially UNSAT.

N2_product_cannot_dom_Bell.note: Confirms dominance encoding correctly rejects inverted ordering.

N3_Werner07_dom_Werner03_is_SAT.test: Werner-0.7 dom Werner-0.3 should be SAT
N3_Werner07_dom_Werner03_is_SAT.note: Higher-p Werner state strictly dominates lower-p Werner state on both axes.

B1_Werner03_cannot_dom_Werner07.note: Lower-p Werner cannot dominate higher-p Werner. UNSAT expected.

B2_equal_MI_strict_dom_UNSAT.encoding: mi_x==mi_y AND ic_x>ic_y AND mi_x>mi_y -> UNSAT
B2_equal_MI_strict_dom_UNSAT.note: States with equal MI cannot strict-dominate on the MI axis.

B3_GHZ_Bell_tie_check.note: GHZ reduced to 2-qubit (tracing out C) collapses to Bell-like structure. If tied, both dominance directions are UNSAT — DAG correctly makes them incomparable. This documents the GHZ/Bell tie that Sim C (3-qubit DAG) is designed to break.
